key_insight: Optimizing CVT growth conditions can suppress the electrically active D* defect in CrSBr by up to an order of magnitude, enabling defect-controlled crystal growth.
engineering_relevance: Defect tuning via precursor stoichiometry and growth temperature offers a practical route to improve the consistency and electronic/magneto-electrical performance of CrSBr devices.
method: Correlated CVT growth conditions with defect populations using conductive AFM (CAFM).
Varied precursor stoichiometry and absolute growth temperatures while keeping the temperature gradient fixed.
Combined thermodynamic modeling and DFT to identify the likely atomic origin of D*.
evidence_signal: CAFM measurements show D* defect densities drop by up to 10× under sulfur- and bromine-rich, lower-temperature growth conditions.
watch_item: The D* assignment is supported by modeling and calculations, but the exact atomic structure remains inferential rather than directly imaged.
paper_title: Growth-controlled suppression of electrically active defects in CrSBr
